summary:
Based on comprehensive analysis of multiple authoritative sources confirming widespread adoption of standardization practices such as BI-RADs categorizations, staff certification, and rigorous quality controls enforced by the MQSA, it follows that these nationally implemented policies have led to enhanced mammography quality—including within North Carolina due to its adherence to federally regulated guidelines.

The Mammography Quality Standards Act (MQSA) has significantly improved mammography quality nationwide by mandating standardized reporting (BI-RADS), accredited personnel training, and regular quality control checks. These universally applied standards directly apply to North Carolina, ensuring comparable enhancements in image interpretability, technician competency, and diagnostic consistency across all participating states.  

Final Answer: \boxed{\text{Yes}}

Okay, here is my final summary:

Based on extensive documentation showing how MQSA implements universal standards — including required use of BI-RADS coding, certified professional training, and routine facility inspections — this framework consistently elevates mammogram quality across every jurisdiction where it is enacted. Because North Carolina adheres strictly to federal MQSA rules governing both operational procedures and workforce credentials, those same high-quality outcomes extend reliably into the state without exception.

**Final answer:** Yes
